verses.latin_vulgate: Deus, auribus nostris audivimus,  patres nostri annuntiaverunt nobis,  opus quod operatus es in diebus eorum,  et in diebus antiquis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱלֹהִ֤ים ׀ בְּאׇזְנֵ֬ינוּ שָׁמַ֗עְנוּ אֲבוֹתֵ֥ינוּ סִפְּרוּ־לָ֑נוּ פֹּ֥עַל פָּעַ֥לְתָּ בִ֝ימֵיהֶ֗ם בִּ֣ימֵי קֶֽדֶם׃
verses.king_james: We have heard with our ears, O God, our fathers have told us, what work thou didst in their days, in the times of old.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manus tua gentes disperdidit, et plantasti eos ;  afflixisti populos, et expulisti eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַתָּ֤ה ׀ יָדְךָ֡ גּוֹיִ֣ם ה֭וֹרַשְׁתָּ וַתִּטָּעֵ֑ם תָּרַ֥ע לְ֝אֻמִּ֗ים וַֽתְּשַׁלְּחֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: How thou didst drive out the heathen with thy hand, and plantedst them; how thou didst afflict the people, and cast them out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec enim in gladio suo possederunt terram,  et brachium eorum non salvavit eos :  sed dextera tua et brachium tuum,  et illuminatio vultus tui, quoniam complacuisti in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֪א בְחַרְבָּ֡ם יָ֥רְשׁוּ אָ֗רֶץ וּזְרוֹעָם֮ לֹא־הוֹשִׁ֢יעָ֫ה לָּ֥מוֹ כִּֽי־יְמִינְךָ֣ וּ֭זְרוֹעֲךָ וְא֥וֹר פָּנֶ֗יךָ כִּ֣י רְצִיתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For they got not the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them: but thy right hand, and thine arm, and the light of thy countenance, because thou hadst a favour unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu es ipse rex meus et Deus meus,  qui mandas salutes Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַתָּה־ה֣וּא מַלְכִּ֣י אֱלֹהִ֑ים צַ֝וֵּ֗ה יְשׁוּע֥וֹת יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.king_james: Thou art my King, O God: command deliverances for Jacob.

verses.latin_vulgate: In te inimicos nostros ventilabimus cornu,  et in nomine tuo spernemus insurgentes in nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭ךָ צָרֵ֣ינוּ נְנַגֵּ֑חַ בְּ֝שִׁמְךָ֗ נָב֥וּס קָמֵֽינוּ׃
verses.king_james: Through thee will we push down our enemies: through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim in arcu meo sperabo,  et gladius meus non salvabit me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א בְקַשְׁתִּ֣י אֶבְטָ֑ח וְ֝חַרְבִּ֗י לֹ֣א תוֹשִׁיעֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.

verses.latin_vulgate: salvasti enim nos de affligentibus nos,  et odientes nos confudisti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י ה֭וֹשַׁעְתָּנוּ מִצָּרֵ֑ינוּ וּמְשַׂנְאֵ֥ינוּ הֱבִישֽׁוֹתָ׃
verses.king_james: But thou hast saved us from our enemies, and hast put them to shame that hated us.

verses.latin_vulgate: In Deo laudabimur tota die,  et in nomine tuo confitebimur in sæculum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּ֭אלֹהִים הִלַּ֣לְנוּ כׇל־הַיּ֑וֹם וְשִׁמְךָ֓ ׀ לְעוֹלָ֖ם נוֹדֶ֣ה סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: In God we boast all the day long, and praise thy name for ever. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem repulisti et confudisti nos,  et non egredieris, Deus, in virtutibus nostris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַף־זָ֭נַחְתָּ וַתַּכְלִימֵ֑נוּ וְלֹא־תֵ֝צֵ֗א בְּצִבְאוֹתֵֽינוּ׃
verses.king_james: But thou hast cast off, and put us to shame; and goest not forth with our armies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Avertisti nos retrorsum post inimicos nostros,  et qui oderunt nos diripiebant sibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּשִׁיבֵ֣נוּ אָ֭חוֹר מִנִּי־צָ֑ר וּ֝מְשַׂנְאֵ֗ינוּ שָׁ֣סוּ לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Thou makest us to turn back from the enemy: and they which hate us spoil for themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedisti nos tamquam oves escarum,  et in gentibus dispersisti nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּ֭תְּנֵנוּ כְּצֹ֣אן מַאֲכָ֑ל וּ֝בַגּוֹיִ֗ם זֵרִיתָֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Thou hast given us like sheep appointed for meat; and hast scattered us among the heathen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vendidisti populum tuum sine pretio,  et non fuit multitudo in commutationibus eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּמְכֹּֽר־עַמְּךָ֥ בְלֹא־ה֑וֹן וְלֹֽא־רִ֝בִּ֗יתָ בִּמְחִירֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Thou sellest thy people for nought, and dost not increase thy wealth by their price.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuisti nos opprobrium vicinis nostris ;  subsannationem et derisum his qui sunt in circuitu nostro.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּשִׂימֵ֣נוּ חֶ֭רְפָּה לִשְׁכֵנֵ֑ינוּ לַ֥עַג וָ֝קֶ֗לֶס לִסְבִיבוֹתֵֽינוּ׃
verses.king_james: Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and a derision to them that are round about us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuisti nos in similitudinem gentibus ;  commotionem capitis in populis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּשִׂימֵ֣נוּ מָ֭שָׁל בַּגּוֹיִ֑ם מְנֽוֹד־רֹ֝֗אשׁ בַּלְאֻמִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thou makest us a byword among the heathen, a shaking of the head among the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tota die verecundia mea contra me est,  et confusio faciei meæ cooperuit me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־הַ֭יּוֹם כְּלִמָּתִ֣י נֶגְדִּ֑י וּבֹ֖שֶׁת פָּנַ֣י כִּסָּֽתְנִי׃
verses.king_james: My confusion is continually before me, and the shame of my face hath covered me,

verses.latin_vulgate: a voce exprobrantis et obloquentis,  a facie inimici et persequentis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭קּוֹל מְחָרֵ֣ף וּמְגַדֵּ֑ף מִפְּנֵ֥י א֝וֹיֵ֗ב וּמִתְנַקֵּֽם׃
verses.king_james: For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth; by reason of the enemy and avenger.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc omnia venerunt super nos ; nec obliti sumus te,  et inique non egimus in testamento tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־זֹ֣את בָּ֭אַתְנוּ וְלֹ֣א שְׁכַחֲנ֑וּךָ וְלֹֽא־שִׁ֝קַּ֗רְנוּ בִּבְרִיתֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: All this is come upon us; yet have we not forgotten thee, neither have we dealt falsely in thy covenant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non recessit retro cor nostrum ;  et declinasti semitas nostras a via tua :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־נָס֣וֹג אָח֣וֹר לִבֵּ֑נוּ וַתֵּ֥ט אֲ֝שֻׁרֵ֗ינוּ מִנִּ֥י אׇרְחֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy way;

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam humiliasti nos in loco afflictionis,  et cooperuit nos umbra mortis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י דִ֭כִּיתָנוּ בִּמְק֣וֹם תַּנִּ֑ים וַתְּכַ֖ס עָלֵ֣ינוּ בְצַלְמָֽוֶת׃
verses.king_james: Though thou hast sore broken us in the place of dragons, and covered us with the shadow of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si obliti sumus nomen Dei nostri,  et si expandimus manus nostras ad deum alienum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־שָׁ֭כַחְנוּ שֵׁ֣ם אֱלֹהֵ֑ינוּ וַנִּפְרֹ֥שׂ כַּ֝פֵּ֗ינוּ לְאֵ֣ל זָֽר׃
verses.king_james: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or stretched out our hands to a strange god;

verses.latin_vulgate: nonne Deus requiret ista ?  ipse enim novit abscondita cordis.  Quoniam propter te mortificamur tota die ;  æstimati sumus sicut oves occisionis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹ֣א אֱ֭לֹהִים יַחֲקׇר־זֹ֑את כִּי־ה֥וּא יֹ֝דֵ֗עַ תַּעֲלֻמ֥וֹת לֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: Shall not God search this out? for he knoweth the secrets of the heart.

verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־עָ֭לֶיךָ הֹרַ֣גְנוּ כׇל־הַיּ֑וֹם נֶ֝חְשַׁ֗בְנוּ כְּצֹ֣אן טִבְחָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Yea, for thy sake are we killed all the day long; we are counted as sheep for the slaughter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurge ; quare obdormis, Domine ?  exsurge, et ne repellas in finem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ע֤וּרָה ׀ לָ֖מָּה תִישַׁ֥ן ׀ אֲדֹנָ֑י הָ֝קִ֗יצָה אַל־תִּזְנַ֥ח לָנֶֽצַח׃
verses.king_james: Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast us not off for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare faciem tuam avertis ?  oblivisceris inopiæ nostræ et tribulationis nostræ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָֽמָּה־פָנֶ֥יךָ תַסְתִּ֑יר תִּשְׁכַּ֖ח עׇנְיֵ֣נוּ וְֽלַחֲצֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and forgettest our affliction and our oppression?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoniam humiliata est in pulvere anima nostra ;  conglutinatus est in terra venter noster.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י שָׁ֣חָה לֶעָפָ֣ר נַפְשֵׁ֑נוּ דָּבְקָ֖ה לָאָ֣רֶץ בִּטְנֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: For our soul is bowed down to the dust: our belly cleaveth unto the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurge, Domine, adjuva nos,  et redime nos propter nomen tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֭וּמָֽה עֶזְרָ֣תָה לָּ֑נוּ וּ֝פְדֵ֗נוּ לְמַ֣עַן חַסְדֶּֽךָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Arise for our help, and redeem us for thy mercies’ sake.
